perturbation.original_text: NON -DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT THIS AGREEMENT (the " Agreement ”) is entered into on this ____day of ___________ by and between _________________, located at ___________________ ( the” Disclosing Party” ), and ___________________________ with an address at ______________________ (the “ Receiving Party” ). The Receiving Party hereto desires to participate in discussions regarding ________________________ (the “ Transaction ”). During these discussions, Disclosing Party may share certain proprietary information with the Receiving Party . Therefore, in consideration of the mutual promises and covenants contained in this Agreement, and other good and valuable consideration, the receipt and sufficiency of which is hereby acknowledged, the parties hereto agree as fol lows:
perturbation.changed_text: NON -DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT THIS AGREEMENT (the " Agreement ”) is entered into on this ____day of ___________ by and between _________________, located at ___________________ ( the” Data Provider” ), and ___________________________ with an address at ______________________ (the “ Data Recipient” ). The Data Recipient hereto desires to participate in discussions regarding ________________________ (the “ Project” ). During these discussions, Data Provider may share certain proprietary information with the Data Recipient . Therefore, in consideration of the mutual promises and covenants contained in this Agreement, and other good and valuable consideration, the receipt and sufficiency of which is hereby acknowledged, the parties hereto agree as fol lows:
perturbation.explanation: The terms 'Disclosing Party', 'Receiving Party', and 'Transaction' are replaced with 'Data Provider', 'Data Recipient', and 'Project' respectively. While arguably similar, the original definitions are not updated throughout the document, creating confusion about the actual obligations. The text refers to 'Disclosing Party' later on, leading to a contradiction when it is no longer defined as such. The contradiction arises because the rest of the NDA continues to use the original terms ('Disclosing Party', 'Receiving Party', 'Transaction'), even though the initial definitions were changed.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1. Definition of Confidential Information . (a) For purposes of this Agreement, “ Confidential Information ” means any data or information that is proprietary to the Disclosing Party and not generally known to the public, whether in tangible or intangible form, in whatever medium provided, whether unmodified or modified by Receiving Party or its Representatives (as defined herein), whenever and however disclosed, including, but not limited to: (i) any marketing strategies, plans, financial information, or projections, operations, sales estimates, business plans and performance results relating to the past, present or future business activities of such party, its affiliates, subsidiaries and affiliated companies; (ii) plans for products or services, and customer or supplier lists; (iii) any scientific or technical information, invention, design, process, procedure, formula, improvement, technology or method; (iv) any concepts, reports, data, know -how, works -in-progress, designs, development tools, specificati ons, computer software, source code, object code, flow charts, databases, inventions, information and trade secrets; (v) any other information that should reasonably be recognized as confidential information of the Disclosing Party ; and (vi) any informatio n generated by the Receiving Party or by its Representatives that contains, reflects, or is derived from any of the foregoing . Confidential Information need not be novel, unique, patentable, copyrightable or constitute a trade secret in order to be design ated Confidential Information. The Receiving Party acknowledges that the Confidential Information is proprietary to the Disclosing Party, has been developed and obtained through great efforts by the Disclosing Party and that Disclosing Party regards all of its Confidential Information as trade secrets . (b) Notwithstanding anything in the foregoing to the contrary, Confidential Information shall not include information which: a) was lawfully possessed, as evidenced by the Receiving Party’s records, by the Receiving Party prior to receiving the Confidential Information from the Disclosing Party; (b) becomes rightfully known by the Receiving Party from a third -party source not under an obligation to Disclosing Party to maintain confidentiality; (c) is generally known by the public through no fault of or failure to act by the Receiving Party inconsistent with its obligations under this Agreement; (d) is required to be disclosed in a judicial or administrative proceeding, or is oth erwise requested or required to be disclosed by law or regulation, although the requirements of paragraph 4 hereof shall apply prior to any disclosure being made; and (e) is or has been independently developed by employees, consultants or agents of the Rec eiving Party without violation of the terms of this Agreement , as evidenced by the Receiving Party’s records, and without reference or access to any Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text redefines 'Disclosing Party' as 'Data Provider' and 'Receiving Party' as 'Data Recipient' and 'Transaction' as 'Project'. The contradicted text, specifically section 1, relies on the original definitions of 'Disclosing Party' and 'Receiving Party' to define 'Confidential Information' and outline obligations. Since the original terms are no longer consistently defined throughout the document, and the contradicted text continues to use the original terminology, this creates a direct conflict and in-text contradiction, because the definitions no longer align, leading to ambiguity and potential unenforceability.

perturbation.original_text: Confidential Information need not be novel, unique, patentable, copyrightable or constitute a trade secret in order to be design ated Confidential Information. The Receiving Party acknowledges that the Confidential Information is proprietary to the Disclosing Party, has been developed and obtained through great efforts by the Disclosing Party and that Disclosing Party regards all of its Confidential Information as trade secrets .
perturbation.changed_text: Protected Information need not be novel, unique, patentable, copyrightable or constitute a trade secret in order to be design ated Protected Information. The Data Recipient acknowledges that the Protected Information is proprietary to the Data Provider, has been developed and obtained through great efforts by the Data Provider and that Data Provider regards all of its Protected Information as business intelligence .
perturbation.explanation: Here, 'Confidential Information' is replaced with 'Protected Information', 'Receiving Party' with 'Data Recipient', 'Disclosing Party' with 'Data Provider', and 'trade secrets' with 'business intelligence'. The initial section defined 'Confidential Information', but later clauses still refer to 'Confidential Information,' creating a contradiction. The meaning is further obscured as the Disclosing Party now regards information as business intelligence
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2. Disclosure of Confidential Information . From time to time, the Disclosing Party may disclose Confidential Information to the Receiving Party. The Receiving Party will: (a) limit disclosure of any Confidential Information to its #886871 2 directors, officers, employees, agents or representatives (collectively “ Representatives ”) who have a need to know such Confidential Information in connection with the current or contemplated business relationship between the parties to which this Agreement relates, and only for that purpose; (b) advise its Representatives of the proprietary nature of the Conf idential Information and of the obligations set forth in this Agreement , require such Representatives to be bound by written confidentiality restrictions no less stringent than those contained herein, and assume full liability for acts or omissions by its Representatives that are inconsistent with its obligations under this Agreement; (c) keep all Confidential Information strictly confidential by using a reasonable degree of care, but not less than the degree of care used by it in safeguarding its own confi dential information; and (d) not disclose any Confidential Information received by it to any third parties (except as otherwise provided for herein).
perturbation.justification: The change replaces 'Confidential Information' with 'Protected Information', but the contradicted text still refers to 'Confidential Information'. This creates a direct conflict as the contract now uses a term that is no longer defined in the modified section, rendering obligations related to 'Confidential Information' ambiguous and potentially unenforceable. The change in terminology directly breaks compatibility, leading to uncertainty about which information is actually being protected under the agreement.

perturbation.original_text: From time to time, the Disclosing Party may disclose Confidential Information to the Receiving Party. The Receiving Party will: (a) limit disclosure of any Confidential Information to its #886871 2 directors, officers, employees, agents or representatives (collectively “ Representatives ”) who have a need to know such Confidential Information in connection with the current or contemplated business relationship between the parties to which this Agreement relates, and only for that purpose; (b) advise its Representatives of the proprietary nature of the Conf idential Information and of the obligations set forth in this Agreement , require such Representatives to be bound by written confidentiality restrictions no less stringent than those contained herein, and assume full liability for acts or omissions by its Representatives that are inconsistent with its obligations under this Agreement; (c) keep all Confidential Information strictly confidential by using a reasonable degree of care, but not less than the degree of care used by it in safeguarding its own confi dential information; and (d) not disclose any Confidential Information received by it to any third parties (except as otherwise provided for herein).
perturbation.changed_text: From time to time, the Data Provider may disclose Project Data to the Data Recipient. The Data Recipient will: (a) limit disclosure of any Project Data to its #886871 2 directors, officers, employees, agents or representatives (collectively “ Representatives ”) who have a need to know such Project Data in connection with the current or contemplated business relationship between the parties to which this Agreement relates, and only for that purpose; (b) advise its Representatives of the proprietary nature of the Project Data and of the obligations set forth in this Agreement , require such Representatives to be bound by written confidentiality restrictions no less stringent than those contained herein, and assume full liability for acts or omissions by its Representatives that are inconsistent with its obligations under this Agreement; (c) keep all Project Data strictly confidential by using a reasonable degree of care, but not less than the degree of care used by it in safeguarding its own confi dential information; and (d) not disclose any Project Data received by it to any third parties (except as otherwise provided for herein).
perturbation.explanation: 'Disclosing Party', 'Receiving Party', and 'Confidential Information' are replaced with 'Data Provider', 'Data Recipient', and 'Project Data'. However, section 2(c) maintains 'confidential information', creating a contradiction. It's unclear what standard of care applies to 'confidential information' vs. 'Project Data'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (c) keep all Project Data strictly confidential by using a reasonable degree of care, but not less than the degree of care used by it in safeguarding its own confi dential information;
perturbation.justification: The change replaces 'Confidential Information' with 'Project Data' in the primary clause. However, the contradicted text (which is actually the same clause after the edit) retains the term 'confidential information'. This creates a contradiction because the clause now refers to both 'Project Data' and 'confidential information' without clarifying if they are the same or distinct, leading to ambiguity about the required standard of care. The explanation states: "It's unclear what standard of care applies to 'confidential information' vs. 'Project Data'." which means it meets the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction', specifically that 'the contract uses multiple terms interchangeably without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations'.

perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Party agrees to use the Confidential Information solely i n connection with the current or contemplated business relationship between the parties and not for any purpose other than as authorized by this Agreement without the prior written consent of an authorized representative of the Disclosing Party. No other right or license, whether expressed or implied, in the Confidential Information is granted to the Receiving Party hereunder. Title to the Confidential Information will remain solely in the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.changed_text: The Data Recipient agrees to use the Project Data solely i n connection with the current or contemplated business relationship between the parties and not for any purpose other than as authorized by this Agreement without the prior written consent of an authorized representative of the Data Provider. No other right or license, whether expressed or implied, in the Intellectual Property is granted to the Data Recipient hereunder. Title to the Trade Secrets will remain solely in the Data Provider.
perturbation.explanation: 'Receiving Party' is now 'Data Recipient', 'Confidential Information' is now 'Project Data', 'Disclosing Party' is now 'Data Provider', 'Confidential Information' is now 'Intellectual Property', and 'Confidential Information' is now 'Trade Secrets'. The document uses inconsistent terminology. 'Project Data' and 'Intellectual Property' now are used to identify the same definition, which leads to ambiguity, especially given other sections continue to speak of Confidential Information
perturbation.contradicted_text: 7. Return of Confidential Information . Receiving Party shall immediately return and redeliver to Disclosing Party all tangible mater ial embodying any Confidential Information provided hereunder and all notes, summaries, memoranda, drawings, manuals, records, excerpts or derivative information deriving therefrom, and all other documents or materials (“Notes”) (and all copies of any of t he foregoing, including “copies” that have been converted to computerized media in the form of image, data , word processing , or other types of files either manually or by image capture) based on or including any Confidential Information, in whatever form o f storage or retrieval, upon the earlier of (i) the completion or termination of the dealings between the parties contemplated hereunder; (ii) the termination of this Agreement; or (iii) at such time as the Disclosing Party may so request ; provided however that the Receiving Party may retain such of its documents as is necessary to enable it to comply with its reasonable document retention policies . Alternatively, the Receiving Party, with the written consent of the Disclosing Party may (or in the case of Notes, at the Receiving Party’s option) immediately destroy any of the foregoing embodying Confidential Information (or the reasonably nonrecoverable data erasure of computerized data) and, upon request, certify in writing such destruction by an authorized officer of the Receiving Party supervising the destruction).
perturbation.justification: The changed text redefines 'Receiving Party' as 'Data Recipient', 'Confidential Information' as 'Project Data', 'Disclosing Party' as 'Data Provider', 'Confidential Information' as 'Intellectual Property', and 'Confidential Information' as 'Trade Secrets'. However, the contradicted text refers to 'Receiving Party', 'Disclosing Party', and 'Confidential Information'. Since the document now uses different terms to refer to the same concepts without explicitly stating the equivalence, a contradiction arises. Specifically, the obligation in the contradicted text to return 'Confidential Information' becomes unclear because the modified text implies that 'Confidential Information' might also be 'Project Data' or 'Intellectual Property' or 'Trade Secrets', but it's not explicitly defined as such, leading to ambiguity about what needs to be returned. This constitutes a Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction as per the definitions.

perturbation.original_text: Receiving Party shall immediately return and redeliver to Disclosing Party all tangible mater ial embodying any Confidential Information provided hereunder and all notes, summaries, memoranda, drawings, manuals, records, excerpts or derivative information deriving therefrom, and all other documents or materials (“Notes”) (and all copies of any of t he foregoing, including “copies” that have been converted to computerized media in the form of image, data , word processing , or other types of files either manually or by image capture) based on or including any Confidential Information, in whatever form o f storage or retrieval, upon the earlier of (i) the completion or termination of the dealings between the parties contemplated hereunder; (ii) the termination of this Agreement; or (iii) at such time as the Disclosing Party may so request ; provided however that the Receiving Party may retain such of its documents as is necessary to enable it to comply with its reasonable document retention policies . Alternatively, the Receiving Party, with the written consent of the Disclosing Party may (or in the case of Notes, at the Receiving Party’s option) immediately destroy any of the foregoing embodying Confidential Information (or the reasonably nonrecoverable data erasure of computerized data) and, upon request, certify in writing such destruction by an authorized officer of the Receiving Party supervising the destruction).
perturbation.changed_text: Data Recipient shall immediately return and redeliver to Data Provider all tangible material embodying any Trade Secrets provided hereunder and all notes, summaries, memoranda, drawings, manuals, records, excerpts or derivative information deriving therefrom, and all other documents or materials (“Notes”) (and all copies of any of the foregoing, including “copies” that have been converted to computerized media in the form of image, data , word processing , or other types of files either manually or by image capture) based on or including any Proprietary Information, in whatever form o f storage or retrieval, upon the earlier of (i) the completion or termination of the dealings between the parties contemplated hereunder; (ii) the termination of this Agreement; or (iii) at such time as the Data Provider may so request ; provided however that the Data Recipient may retain such of its documents as is necessary to enable it to comply with its reasonable document retention policies . Alternatively, the Data Recipient, with the written consent of the Data Provider may (or in the case of Notes, at the Data Recipient’s option) immediately destroy any of the foregoing embodying Sensitive Information (or the reasonably nonrecoverable data erasure of computerized data) and, upon request, certify in writing such destruction by an authorized officer of the Data Recipient supervising the destruction).
perturbation.explanation: Here, 'Receiving Party' is replaced with 'Data Recipient', 'Disclosing Party' is replaced with 'Data Provider', 'Confidential Information' is now 'Trade Secrets', 'Confidential Information' is now 'Proprietary Information', and 'Confidential Information' is now 'Sensitive Information'. The document now has different terms to define the same definition, the terms 'Trade Secrets', 'Proprietary Information', and 'Sensitive Information'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8. Notice of Breach . Receiving Party shall notify the Disclosing Party immediately upon discovery of , or suspicion of, (1) any unauth orized use or disclosure of Confidential Information by Recei ving Party or its Representatives ; or (2) any actions by Receiving Party or its Representatives inconsistent with their respective obligations under this Agreement , Receiving Party shall cooperate with any and all efforts of the Disclosing Party to help the Disclosing Party regain possession of Confidential Information and prevent its further unauthorized use.
perturbation.justification: The original text uses the terms 'Receiving Party', 'Disclosing Party', and 'Confidential Information'. The changed text replaces these terms with 'Data Recipient', 'Data Provider', 'Trade Secrets', 'Proprietary Information', and 'Sensitive Information'. The 'Perturbation Explanation' states that these new terms are intended to define the same concepts as the original terms. However, the contradicted text refers to 'Receiving Party', 'Disclosing Party', and 'Confidential Information'. Since the changed text uses different terms for the same concepts, it creates a contradiction because the contradicted text refers to the original terms, not the new terms introduced in the changed text. The 'Misaligned Terminology' definition states that this leads to in-text contradictions when the contract uses multiple terms interchangeably without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: Receiving Party shall notify the Disclosing Party immediately upon discovery of , or suspicion of, (1) any unauth orized use or disclosure of Confidential Information by Recei ving Party or its Representatives ; or (2) any actions by Receiving Party or its Representatives inconsistent with their respective obligations under this Agreement , Receiving Party shall cooperate with any and all efforts of the Disclosing Party to help the Disclosing Party regain possession of Confidential Information and prevent its further unauthorized use.
perturbation.changed_text: Data Recipient shall notify the Data Provider immediately upon discovery of , or suspicion of, (1) any unauth orized use or disclosure of Project Intellect by Data Recipient or its Representatives ; or (2) any actions by Data Recipient or its Representatives inconsistent with their respective obligations under this Agreement , Data Recipient shall cooperate with any and all efforts of the Data Provider to help the Data Provider regain possession of Project Intellect and prevent its further unauthorized use.
perturbation.explanation: The terminology is changed again, 'Receiving Party' to 'Data Recipient', 'Disclosing Party' to 'Data Provider', and 'Confidential Information' to 'Project Intellect'. The document has different terms to define the same definition, now 'Project Intellect' is another term for it. It remains unclear what to do with the Confidential Information, as defined at the start, but now unreferenced
perturbation.contradicted_text: 10. Warranty . NO WARRANTIES ARE MADE BY EITHER PARTY UNDER THIS AGREEMENT WHATSOEVER . The parties acknowledge that although they shall each endeavor to include in the Confidential Information all information that they each believe relevant for the purpose of the evaluatio n of a Transaction, the parties understand that no representation or warranty as to the accuracy or completeness of the Confidential Information is being made by the Disclosing Party. Further, neither party is under any obligation under this Agreement to disclose any Confidential Information it chooses not to disclose. Neither Party hereto shall have any liability to the other party or to the other party’s Representatives resulting from any use of the Confidential Information except with respect to disclo sure of such Confidential Information in violation of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Confidential Information' to 'Project Intellect' creates a contradiction. The contradicted text references 'Confidential Information' and makes stipulations about it, but the changed text now uses 'Project Intellect'. The document now has two terms to describe the same thing without directly addressing the term 'Confidential Information' in the changed text, as stated in the explanation. This creates a contradiction because the original meaning and usage of 'Confidential Information' are now ambiguous, leading to an in-text contradiction as per the definition of Misaligned Terminology.
